steps.title: Identify what $y = 0$ means in context
steps.content: The equation $y = 18 - 5x$ gives the bike's speed $y$ (in feet per second) at time $x$ (seconds after braking). The $x$-intercept is the point where $y = 0$ — that is, where the speed equals 0 feet per second. Speed 0 means the bicycle has come to a complete stop.

steps.title: Solve for the $x$-coordinate of the intercept
steps.content: Set $y = 0$: $0 = 18 - 5x$, so $x = 18/5 = 3.6$ seconds. Because $x$ measures time after braking, this $x$-coordinate represents the number of seconds from when Sheila began braking until the bike stopped.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) Speed before braking would be $y$ at $x = 0$, namely $y = 18$ — that's the $y$-intercept, not the $x$-intercept. Eliminate.
  • B) The rate of decrease in speed is the slope, $5$ ft/s per second — not the $x$-intercept. Eliminate.
  • C) The $x$-coordinate at $y = 0$ is the time to stop. Keep.
  • D) Distance traveled would require integrating speed over time, not reading off an intercept. Eliminate.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
